confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_53455c47-7c06-42fd-9873-56dec2e9f891 read back as archived

evidence: The Utz workspace already contained data/ and model/ folders and was not a Git repo. The Site was created at C:/Users/Andrey/OneDrive - Global Maxima LLC/!Business/!Clients/Utz/.narada with AGENTS.md, config.json, README, chapters, tasks, decisions, KB, inbox, observations, friction, requests, and .ai/inbox-drop plus .ai/inbox-envelopes. JSON parse passed and narada inbox ingest-files --from .ai/inbox-drop dry-run succeeded with zero candidates.
proposal: Capture this as the standard client-service Site bootstrap path: inspect workspace, preserve existing client material, create contained .narada Site, install canonical inbox file-drop, record routing/data-handling guidance, and validate with config parse plus inbox dry-run. Do not initialize Git, mailbox sync, data Site, or ELT Site unless the workspace and operator intent justify them.
payload.recommendation: Add or update a Narada client Site bootstrap template/command so this pattern can be executed directly. Remaining friction: empty directory creation and boilerplate files still require manual setup; the bootstrap command should create durable directories, .ai/inbox-drop, .ai/inbox-envelopes, guidance docs, and validation output in one governed operation.
payload.summary: Creating the Utz client-service Site went substantially cleaner than the earlier Staccato setup because the de-arbitrized pattern had become clear: keep the visible client workspace intact, place Narada governance inside .narada, install canonical inbox file-drop from the start, avoid mailbox/Git assumptions until warranted, and validate immediately.
payload.title: Client Site bootstrap pattern stabilized through Utz setup

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
